indicator_text: To what extent do relevant laws, regulations, policies, and guidance provide a basis for collecting and publishing data on lobbying activities?
section_or_category: Governance pillar / Political Integrity module — primary indicator
scoring_rule: Pillar weight 0.065; module weight 0.075. Composite = elements_score (0-100) x existence_multiplier x extent_multiplier. Existence multipliers: 0 if frameworks do not exist; 0.6 if frameworks are being drafted or not yet implemented; 1 if frameworks exist and are operational. Data-rule multipliers (separate): 0.6 no mention of data; 0.85 non-binding policy/guidance; 0.95 binding policy/regulations/law; 1.0 binding open-data requirement. Extent multipliers: 0.7 most limited extent; 0.85 mid-level; 0.9 between mid and highest (where >3 options); 1 highest. Elements scored 0/0.5/1 ('No'/'Partially'/'Yes'); element groups e1, e2, e3 sum to 100, with optional 'eb' negative-scoring group up to -20. Researchers also asked, where no framework exists, 'are there alternative norms or customs that play this role in the country?'
source_quote: To what extent do relevant laws, regulations, policies, and guidance provide a basis for collecting and publishing data on lobbying activities? (papers/text/GlobalDataBarometer_2022__report.txt:5694-5698) — full appendix indicator name 'Lobbying register', module 'Political Integrity', type 'Primary', Weight in Pillar 0.065, Weight in Module 0.075.
notes: The Governance-pillar indicator paired with the Availability indicator below. Together these are the only two GDB primary indicators specifically on lobbying. Variable-name convention follows the GDB pattern (cf. worked example G.GOVERNANCE.DPL.e for Data Protection Law); the actual variable prefix in the GDB dataset is not printed in the report. Detailed sub-questions live in the research handbook at https://handbook.globaldatabarometer.org/2021/, not in this report. Across 109 countries, 'lobbying registers' had the lowest mean score among sectoral governance indicators (papers/text/GlobalDataBarometer_2022__report.txt:1236-1238).

indicator_text: To what extent is lobby register information available as open data?
section_or_category: Availability pillar / Political Integrity module — primary indicator
scoring_rule: Pillar weight 0.0570; module weight 0.095. Availability existence multipliers: 0 'data is not available online'; 0.5 'data is available, but not as a result of government action'; 1 'data is available from government, or because of government actions'. Element score (0-100) is built from a common open-data checklist (~60% weight: structured data, machine-readable, bulk, open license, free, timely, with accessible exploration tools) plus dataset-specific elements (~40%). Extent multipliers as above. Open-definition criteria assessed: 'Online, Free, Machine Readable, Open License, Bulk data' plus 'presence of accessible tools to explore available data' (papers/text/GlobalDataBarometer_2022__report.txt:2053-2054).
source_quote: To what extent is lobby register information available as open data? (papers/text/GlobalDataBarometer_2022__report.txt:5850-5852) — full appendix indicator name 'Lobbying data', module 'Political Integrity', type 'Primary', Weight in Pillar 0.0570, Weight in Module 0.095. Topline cross-country result: "Lobbying data | 19 [available online] | 4 [open data] | 4.7 [mean quality score] | 5.19 [mean openness score]" (papers/text/GlobalDataBarometer_2022__report.txt:2427).
notes: Of the 109 surveyed countries, only 19 had any lobby register information online and only 4 met the open-data definition; 'just 17.4% of countries providing data online, and only 4 offering open data' (papers/text/GlobalDataBarometer_2022__report.txt:2502-2504). This is FOCAL's cited figure ("only 19 of 109 surveyed countries had a lobbyist register available online"). Canada flagged as Regional + Global Leader on Lobbying data; Taiwan as Regional Leader on Lobbying register. The data-availability score thus rolls up at least these checklist-style sub-elements (per the methodology); finer sub-question text is in the handbook, not the report.

indicator_text: To what extent is political integrity data interoperable across different political integrity datasets, as well as other datasets associated with relevant information flows?
section_or_category: Capability pillar / Political Integrity module — primary indicator (cross-cuts lobbying, political finance, asset declarations, public consultation, RTI)
scoring_rule: Pillar weight 0.08; module weight 0.075. Capabilities existence multipliers use 3- or 4-point scale (0 / 0.8 / 1, or 0 / 0.6 / 0.8 / 1). Same element/extent structure as other indicators.
source_quote: Political integrity interoperability | Political Integrity | Primary | To what extent is political integrity data interoperable across different political integrity datasets, as well as other datasets associated with relevant information flows? | 0.08 | 0.075 (papers/text/GlobalDataBarometer_2022__report.txt:5759-5763).
notes: Not lobbying-only, but explicitly listed by the report as one of the things that 'ties together' lobbying with political-finance and asset disclosure datasets via 'common identifiers' (papers/text/GlobalDataBarometer_2022__report.txt:2487-2491). Included here because the lobbying availability score for any country is partly contextualized by whether common identifiers exist linking lobbyists to companies/officials. Borderline — extract only if 'lobbying-transparency module' is read broadly to include cross-cutting integrity-data interoperability.
